goal: Import client's 6-DOF arm URDF cleanly, verify articulation, park it on the floor.
thoughts: Last week's floating mess = usually missing inertia + fix_base=False. Use isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf via import_robot with fix_base=True and merge_fixed_joints=True. verify_import catches dropped joint limits, bad mesh scales, base_link offset — the three gotchas that burn billable hours.
code:
import_robot(
    urdf_path="<ABS_URDF_PATH>",        # /home/raj/client_project/arm_v3.urdf
    dest_path="<STAGE_PRIM_PATH>",        # /World/ArmV3
    fix_base=True,
    merge_fixed_joints=True,
    self_collision=False,
    convex_decomposition=False
)

verify_import(prim_path="<STAGE_PRIM_PATH>")

run_stage_analysis()  # confirm DOF==6, articulation rooted correctly

# If sunk into floor: base_link origin ≠ mount point. Lift with:
# set_attribute(prim_path="<STAGE_PRIM_PATH>", attr_name="xformOp:translate", value=[0,0,<BASE_OFFSET>])
failure_modes: URDF path relative or ~-expanded — kit will not resolve
Missing <inertial> tags — arm flops under gravity
Mesh scale mismatch (URDF in m, STL in mm) — giant or invisible arm
Gazebo-only tags (<gazebo>) quietly ignored — do not treat as bug
